Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for NorthWind-related correspondence using search_emails with query "NorthWind" (or similar). Discovers the full email trail: Craig Nguyen's piano damage report, Victor Huang's security requirements and breach escalation, Pam Kowalski's formal account escalation to David, Marcus Thorne's insurance rider email, Catalina's internal updates, and Pam's original Denver relocation request.
OE 2: Read Craig Nguyen's piano damage email (email_email_1f1459bff84c) using get_email_by_id. Discovers: Steinway Model B scratched during April 11 extraction at 2147 Humboldt St Denver, 4.5-5 inch scratch on bass side rim near keyboard cheek block, finish disruption but no structural crack, crew members Luis Romero and Ben Hall, sent to blessing.okafor@moveops.com with CC to claims@keymove-specialty.com.
OE 3: Read Victor Huang's security breach escalation email (email_email_f02adf9dc632) using get_email_by_id. Discovers: chain-of-custody breach - movers arrived and began loading before the 9:00 AM IT security escort arrived, equipment handled without NorthWind authorization, shipment placed into overnight warehouse storage (explicitly prohibited). Victor demands incident response owner, written timeline, and data handling statement. Sent to chloe.vance@moveops.com, CC catalina.dubois@moveops.com.
OE 4: Read Pam Kowalski's formal escalation email (email_email_7168baed8438) using get_email_by_id. Discovers: NorthWind is evaluating whether MoveOps can continue to support the account. Cites both Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach and Emilia Cruz piano damage. References Catalina's broken April 14 recovery promise. Mentions PathFinder Logistics as competitor. Sent to david.chen@moveops.com, CC victor.huang@northwindtech.com.
OE 5: Read Marcus Thorne's insurance rider email (email_email_99e10a978b48) using get_email_by_id. Discovers: KeyMove added $1,200 insurance claim rider for the Emilia Cruz Steinway damage, Marcus recommends processing it, references Craig Nguyen's April 11 damage email and Blessing's walkup assessment admission. This confirms the insurance claim status for the piano damage.
OE 6: Search Slack #operations channel (C006) for NorthWind-related messages using conversations_search_messages with search_query "NorthWind" (or similar) and filter_in_channel "C006". Discovers: Chloe's chain-of-custody failure timeline reconstruction (movers arrived 7 AM, escort at 9 AM, servers warehoused overnight), Chloe's finding that the tracking ticket was closed without a signed custody log, Blessing's admission about the piano walkup assessment, and Catalina's overload warning about six active NorthWind relocations.
OE 7: Search CRM for NorthWind company record using crm_search_companies with name "NorthWind" (or similar). Discovers: company_northwind - "Enterprise manufacturing tech. 5 relocations. Had quality/timeline issues. Catalina's account." Type: Customer.
OE 8: List CRM engagements for NorthWind using crm_list_engagements with company_ids ["company_northwind"]. Discovers: eng_northwind_denver_recovery_context - account recovery context note documenting the $60K contract for 5 relocations, Q1 service failures (5/10 CSAT), and the Denver expansion as a critical relationship test.
OE 9: Search Airtable Relocations table for NorthWind records using airtable_search_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name "Relocations", field_name "Company", value "NorthWind Technologies". Discovers both relocation records: recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver (Chicago->Denver, In Progress, Steinway piano, Coordinator: Blessing Okafor) and recRajPatelChicagoDenver (Chicago->Denver, In Progress, server lab with chain-of-custody requirements, Coordinator: Blessing Okafor). May also find recRajPatelAuditNote0411 documenting the escort timing mismatch.
OE 10: Search Linear for NorthWind-related issues using linear_list_issues with query "NorthWind" (or similar). Discovers: issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind (DOT Docs for Raj Patel lithium UPS, state: done) and linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1 (NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations, priority 1). Agent uses this to identify what already has ticket coverage vs what's missing.
OE 11: Search QuickBooks for KeyMove bills to determine insurance claim status using quickbooks_search_bills (or quickbooks_search_vendors followed by quickbooks_get_bill). Discovers: BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417 (DocNumber: KM-44192-ICR), $1,200 total, description "Insurance claim rider for Emilia Cruz Steinway piano scratch during stairwell extraction", vendor KeyMove Specialty Transport (VEND-KEYMOVE-001), account: Claims & Remediation Expense.
OE 12: Update both NorthWind relocation records in Airtable using airtable_update_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id "tblRelocations01", records array containing updates for both recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver (status notes reflecting piano damage details, $1,200 insurance claim processed, Pam's escalation) and recRajPatelChicagoDenver (status notes reflecting chain-of-custody breach, Victor's unresolved demands, Pam's escalation).
OE 13: Create a Linear issue for the Emilia Cruz piano damage claim using linear_create_issue with team "team_operations" (or team_relocations), title referencing Emilia Cruz piano damage / NorthWind / insurance claim tracking (or similar), and description covering the April 11 damage, KeyMove $1,200 rider, and client-facing follow-up needed. This is the client-facing item identified as lacking a dedicated ticket during investigation.
OE 14: Send email to Chloe from Blessing using send_email with sender "blessing.okafor@moveops.com", recipients ["chloe.vance@moveops.com"], subject referencing NorthWind Denver moves status (or similar), and content summarizing: (1) Emilia Cruz - piano damage details, $1,200 insurance claim processed, no dedicated follow-up ticket until now; (2) Raj Patel - chain-of-custody breach, Victor's demands, DOT docs completed but breach response unclear; (3) Account-level - Pam's formal escalation to David citing both incidents, PathFinder threat, retention at risk. Covers what's resolved, what's open, and what needs Chloe's attention before April 28.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

6 The email to Chloe mentions the $1,200 insurance claim rider from KeyMove Specialty Transport for the piano damage.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✗✗ 67%
✓Run #1The email states: 'KeyMove added a $1,200 insurance claim rider for the scratch (Marcus flagged this in ops channel).' Both $1,200 and KeyMove are referenced in the email content.
✓Run #2The email explicitly states 'KeyMove added a $1,200 insurance claim rider to their bill (Marcus Thorne confirmed and flagged the pattern this creates on the NorthWind margin).
✓Run #3The send_email content explicitly states 'KeyMove billed us a $1,200 insurance claim rider (QuickBooks bill KM-44192-ICR, due April 24)' which directly references the $1,200 amount and KeyMove.
✓Run #4The email content explicitly states: 'KeyMove submitted a $1,200 insurance claim rider (Bill KM-44192-ICR, filed April 17, due April 24).' Both the $1,200 amount and KeyMove reference are present.
✗Run #5The email mentions '$1,200' only in the context of the chain-of-custody protocol line item on the NorthWind invoice — not as a KeyMove Specialty Transport insurance claim rider (BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417, DocNumber KM-44192-ICR). The agent never searched QuickBooks bills and did not identify this insurance claim.
✗Run #6The email content does not mention any $1,200 insurance claim rider. The agent never used QuickBooks tools, so it never found the BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417 bill. The email only references that the 'reimbursement/repair path' is undecided, with no financial specifics.
7 The email to Chloe mentions the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach, specifically that the movers arrived before the scheduled IT security escort or that equipment was loaded without NorthWind authorization (or similar breach description).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email describes: 'SentinelChain crew arrived at 7:00 AM and loaded the server lab...before the NorthWind IT security escort arrived at the confirmed 9:00 AM time. Equipment was then placed into overnight warehouse storage at SentinelChain's South San Francisco facility.' Early arrival and unauthorized loading are clearly described.
